3vijLre760w.0: ...on the planet. Some can trace their origins to over 70 million years ago, back to a time
3vijLre760w.1: when dinosaurs still roamed the Earth. While the giant reptiles have disappeared, rainforests
3vijLre760w.2: continue to thrive, growing on every continent except Antarctica. Two types of rainforests
3vijLre760w.3: are scattered across the globe, temperate and tropical. Temperate rainforests are mainly
3vijLre760w.4: found in the mid-latitudes, often near cooler, coastal, mountainous regions. Tropical rainforests
3vijLre760w.5: are primarily located in warmer climates, between the Tropic of Cancer and the Tropic
3vijLre760w.6: of Capricorn. As their names imply, temperate and tropical rainforests are the wettest forests
3vijLre760w.7: on Earth, receiving up to about 33 feet of rain per year. This precipitation plays a
3vijLre760w.8: critical role in creating an exceptionally lush and biologically diverse habitat.
